name: ChEMBL Web Services
description: REST API for bioactive molecules, targets, and bioassays.

name: ClinicalTrials.gov API
description: API for querying clinical trial metadata and results.

name: Ensembl REST API
description: API for genomic annotations, variants, genes, and comparative genomics.

name: KEGG REST API
description: API for accessing KEGG pathways, compounds, genes, and reactions.

name: NCBI E-utilities
description: Unified APIs for accessing NCBI databases (Gene, GEO, SRA, PubChem, etc).

name: Open Targets Platform API
description: API for target–disease associations integrating genetics, genomics, and drug data.

name: PubMed E-utilities (esearch/efetch)
description: APIs for searching and retrieving biomedical literature from PubMed.

name: UniProt REST API
description: Programmatic access to protein sequence and functional annotation data.

name: 1000 Genomes Project
description: Reference panel of human genetic variation from 2,504 individuals across 26 populations.

name: BACE
description: Binary classification and regression dataset for β-secretase 1 (BACE-1) inhibitor binding affinity.

name: BEAT AML
description: Functional ex vivo drug sensitivity measurements paired with genomics for acute myeloid leukemia.

name: BindingDB Curated Sets
description: Curated binding affinity datasets for protein–ligand interaction benchmarking.

name: Cancer Therapeutics Response Portal (CTRP)
description: Drug sensitivity profiles across ~900 cancer cell lines for >400 compounds.

name: ClinTox
description: Clinical toxicity dataset contrasting FDA-approved drugs with those that failed clinical trials due to toxicity.

name: CPTAC (Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium)
description: Multi-omic proteogenomic datasets for multiple cancer types linking proteomics with genomics.

name: CrossDocked2020
description: Large-scale dataset for structure-based virtual screening.

name: FLIP (Fitness Landscape Inference for Proteins)
description: Benchmark collection of protein fitness landscape datasets for evaluating protein ML models.

name: GuacaMol
description: Benchmark suite for generative molecular design models.

name: LINCS L1000
description: Gene expression profiles (978 landmark genes) for >20,000 chemical and genetic perturbations across cell lines.

name: MoleculeNet
description: Benchmark datasets for molecular machine learning.

name: MOSES
description: Benchmarking platform for molecular generation models.

name: OGB (Open Graph Benchmark)
description: Large-scale graph ML benchmark suite including biological datasets such as ogbl-ppa (protein-protein associations) and ogbg-molhiv.

name: OpenBioLink
description: Benchmark datasets for biological knowledge graph completion.

name: PharmGKB
description: Curated pharmacogenomics dataset linking genetic variants to drug response phenotypes across thousands of drugs.

name: PK-DB
description: Open database of experimental pharmacokinetics (PK) and ADME data from clinical and preclinical studies.

name: PRISM
description: Cancer drug sensitivity profiling of >4,500 drugs across >900 cancer cell lines using pooled-cell-line barcoding.

name: ProteinGym
description: Large-scale benchmark of deep mutational scanning assays for evaluating protein fitness landscape models.

name: QM9
description: Quantum chemistry properties for 134K stable small organic molecules computed at DFT level.

name: scIB (Single-cell Integration Benchmarks)
description: Comprehensive benchmarking framework for single-cell data integration methods.

name: SIDER (Side Effect Resource)
description: Database of 1,430 approved drugs with their recorded adverse drug reactions across 27 system-organ classes.

name: Tabula Muris
description: Comprehensive single-cell atlas of 20 mouse organs and tissues, enabling cross-tissue and cross-species comparisons.

name: Tabula Sapiens
description: Comprehensive human single-cell atlas of ~500K cells from 24 organs and tissues across multiple donors.

name: TAPE (Tasks Assessing Protein Embeddings)
description: Benchmark suite of five biologically meaningful semi-supervised learning tasks for evaluating protein representations.

name: The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA)
description: Comprehensive multi-omics (genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, methylation) dataset for 33 cancer types across ~11,000 patients.

name: Therapeutics Data Commons (TDC)
description: Unified benchmark suite covering ADMET, drug-target interaction, drug response, and more.

name: Tox21
description: 12,707 compounds tested in 12 nuclear receptor and stress-response pathway biochemical assays for toxicity prediction.

name: UK Biobank
description: Large-scale biomedical database of ~500K participants with genetic, imaging, and health data for population genetics and disease studies.

name: 10x Genomics Dataset
description: Collection of single-cell datasets.

name: AlphaFold Protein Structure Database
description: 3D protein structure predictions.

name: BindingDB
description: Compounds and target database.

name: BioCyc
description: Collection of pathway/genome databases across thousands of organisms.

name: BioGRID
description: Protein, genetic, and chemical interactions.

name: Cancer Cell Line Encyclopedia
description: Database of ~1000 cancer cell lines.

name: Catalogue Of Somatic Mutations In Cancer (COSMIC)
description: Resource on somatic mutations in cancers.

name: CATH database
description: Hierarchical classification of protein domain structures.

name: cBioPortal
description: Cancer genomics database; aggregating many patient datasets.

name: CellMiner Cross Database (CellMinerCDB)
description: Integrates multiple cancer cell line databases.

name: ChEBI
description: Database focused on small chemical compounds.

name: ChEMBL
description: Bioactive molecules with drug-like properties.

name: ChemSpider
description: Chemical structure database.

name: ClinicalTrials.gov
description: Privately and publicly funded clinical studies.

name: Comparative Toxicogenomics Database
description: Chemical-gene interactions, chemical-disease and gene-disease associations, chemical-phenotype associations.

name: Critical Assessment of Structure Prediction (CASP)
description: Assessing methods for protein structure prediction.

name: CZ CELLxGENE
description: Single-cell dataset repository and interactive explorer from the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.

name: Davis kinase inhibitors DB
description: Experimental kinase inhibitor binding affinity dataset for protein–ligand interaction research.

name: Dependency Map (DepMap)
description: CRISPR-Cas9 screens in cancer cell lines.

name: DGIdb
description: Drug-gene interactions and the druggable genome.

name: DISEASES
description: Gene–disease association database integrating evidence from text mining, curated databases, and experimental data.

name: DisGeNET
description: Database of gene-disease associations integrating expert-curated and GWAS data.

name: DRKG
description: Large-scale biological knowledge graph for drug discovery.

name: Drug Mechanism Database (DrugMechDB)
description: Mechanisms of action from drug to disease.

name: Drug Repurposing Hub
description: Collections of drug repurposing data (drug, MoA, target, etc).

name: DrugBank
description: Database of drugs and targets (University of Alberta).

name: DrugCentral
description: Online drug compendium with drug mode of action and indication information.

name: DrugTargetCommons
description: Community platform for curating and integrating experimental bioactivity data across drugs and targets.

name: ENCODE
description: Encyclopedia of DNA Elements; regulatory and functional genomic elements across the genome.

name: Ensembl
description: Genome browser and annotation database for vertebrate and other eukaryotic genomes.

name: EU Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials DB (EudraCT)
description: European clinical trial database.

name: FANTOM5
description: Functional annotation of mammalian genome; comprehensive atlas of active enhancers, promoters, and transcription start sites across human and mouse cell types.

name: GenBank
description: NCBI's database of genetic sequences.

name: Gene Expression Omnibus
description: Public functional genomics database.

name: Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC)
description: Drug sensitivity for ~1000 human cancer cell lines and hundreds of compounds.

name: gnomAD
description: Genome Aggregation Database; genetic variation from large-scale sequencing projects.

name: Hetionet
description: Heterogeneous network integrating genes, diseases, drugs, pathways, and more.

name: HIPPIE
description: Human protein-protein interaction database.

name: HMDB (Human Metabolome Database)
description: Comprehensive database of small molecule metabolites found in the human body.

name: Human Cell Atlas
description: Open global atlas of all cells in the human body.

name: Human Genome Resources at NCBI
description: Database for genomics, proteomics, transcriptomics, and systems biology.

name: Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO)
description: Standardized vocabulary of phenotypic abnormalities in human disease, linking genes, variants, and clinical features.

name: ICD10
description: International Classification of Diseases, 10th revision.

name: IntAct
description: Open-source molecular interaction database and analysis system from EMBL-EBI.

name: InterPro
description: Protein families, domains, and functional sites database integrating 14 member databases including Pfam and PROSITE.

name: JASPAR
description: Database of transcription factor binding profiles.

name: KEGG COMPOUND
description: Collection of small molecules and biopolymers.

name: KEGG DRUG
description: Comprehensive, approved drug information.

name: KEGG PATHWAY
description: Collection of pathway maps.

name: Kinase Inhibitor Bioactivity Data (KIBA)
description: Integrated bioactivity scores for kinase inhibitors combining Ki, Kd, and IC50 measurements.

name: LIPID MAPS
description: Database of lipids.

name: MassBank
description: Open source databases and tools for mass spectrometry reference spectra.

name: MGnify
description: Resource for metagenomic and metatranscriptomic data.

name: MIMIC-IV
description: Freely accessible critical care database.

name: miRBase
description: Reference repository for microRNA gene annotations, sequences, and experimentally validated targets.

name: MoNA MassBank of North America
description: Meta-database of metabolite mass spectra, metadata, and associated compounds.

name: MSigDB (Molecular Signatures Database)
description: Curated gene sets derived from pathways and biological processes.

name: NCI60
description: Focuses on 60 cancer cell lines and many drugs.

name: NeXtProt
description: Expert knowledge base on human proteins with deep functional annotation, complementary to UniProt.

name: OADB (Observed Antibody Space Database)
description: Database of antibody sequences from immune repertoire sequencing.

name: OMIM (Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man)
description: Comprehensive database of human genes and genetic disorders.

name: Open Targets Platform
description: Systematic target identification and prioritization platform integrating genetics, genomics, and drug data for drug discovery.

name: PathwayCommons
description: Database of pathways and interactions.

name: PDBBind
description: Binding affinity data for biomolecular complexes.

name: Pfam
description: Database of protein families described by multiple sequence alignments and hidden Markov models.

name: PrimeKG
description: Multi-modal precision medicine knowledge graph integrating clinical, genetic, and drug data.

name: PROTEIN DATA BANK (PDB)
description: 3D structures of proteins, nucleic acids, complexes.

name: PubChem
description: One of the largest chemical databases (compounds, genes, and proteins).

name: RCSB Protein Data Bank
description: Repository for structural data of biological molecules.

name: Reactome
description: Expert-curated, peer-reviewed pathway database with detailed reaction mechanisms.

name: RegNetwork
description: Database of gene regulatory networks covering transcription factor–target gene and miRNA–gene interaction data across multiple species.

name: Rfam
description: Database of RNA families with sequence alignments and consensus structures.

name: Rhea
description: Database of chemical reactions.

name: ROADMAP Epigenomics
description: Reference epigenome maps for 111 primary human cell types and tissues, including histone modifications, chromatin accessibility, and DNA methylation.

name: SAbDab
description: Structural Antibody Database containing all antibody structures in the PDB.

name: SIGNOR
description: Database of causal signaling interactions and pathways.

name: Single Cell Expression Atlas
description: Public database for single-cell RNA.

name: Single Cell PORTAL
description: Public database for single-cell RNA.

name: SNAP
description: Dataset of drug-gene interactions.

name: STITCH
description: Chemical-protein interactions.

name: STRING
description: PPI networks for multiple organisms.

name: The Genotype-Tissue Expression (GTEx)
description: Human gene expression and regulation resource.

name: THE HUMAN PROTEIN ATLAS
description: Comprehensive human protein database (cells, tissues, organs).

name: Therapeutic Target Database
description: Drug-target, target-disease, and drug-disease datasets.

name: TRRUST
description: Manually curated database of human and mouse transcriptional regulatory interactions between transcription factors and their target genes.

name: UCSC Genome Browser
description: UCSC's genome browser.

name: Uniclust
description: Clustered protein sequence databases.

name: UniProt
description: Functional information on proteins.

name: UniRef
description: Non-redundant sequence database clustering UniProtKB entries at multiple sequence identity thresholds.

name: WikiPathways
description: Database of biological pathways.

name: ZINC ligand discovery database
description: Free database of commercially-available compounds for virtual screening.

name: AI4Chem/ChemLLM-7B-Chat
description: LLM for chemical & molecular science.

name: AlphaFold3
description: Predicts structures of proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, and their complexes.

name: Ankh
description: Efficient protein language model optimized for downstream prediction tasks including secondary structure, localization, and function annotation.

name: BABEL
description: Cross-modality translation model enabling prediction between scRNA-seq and scATAC-seq profiles without requiring paired single-cell measurements.

name: Basenji
description: Sequential regulatory activity prediction from DNA sequences.

name: BioGPT
description: LLM for biomedical text generation.

name: BiomedCLIP
description: CLIP-based vision-language foundation model for biomedical images and text trained on PubMed figure–caption pairs.

name: BioMedLM
description: 2.7B parameter GPT-2-style language model trained exclusively on biomedical literature from PubMed for biomedical question answering and text generation.

name: Boltz-1
description: Open-source all-atom biomolecular structure prediction model for proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, and their complexes achieving AlphaFold3-level accuracy.

name: Borzoi
description: Extended successor to Enformer for predicting RNA-seq coverage from long genomic sequence windows (524 kb) with improved resolution.

name: BulkFormer
description: Foundation model for bulk RNA-seq data; learns general transcriptomic representations.

name: Caduceus
description: Bidirectional equivariant long-range DNA sequence model based on Mamba.

name: CancerFoundation
description: Single-cell RNA-seq foundation model trained exclusively on a curated dataset of malignant cells to learn cancer-specific embeddings.

name: CellPLM
description: Cell pre-trained language model with inter-cell transformer architecture for diverse single-cell analysis tasks.

name: Chai-1
description: Unified molecular structure prediction model covering proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, and complexes.

name: ChatDrug
description: LLM-based conversational pipeline for drug discovery, using natural language prompts for iterative drug editing and optimization.

name: ChemBERTa-2
description: Chemical embeddings & prediction.

name: chemCPA
description: Compositional perturbation autoencoder for predicting single-cell transcriptional responses to unseen drug perturbations and dose combinations.

name: CHIEF
description: Clinical Histopathology Imaging Evaluation Foundation model integrating histology images and clinical context for pan-cancer analysis.

name: ClawBio
description: Bioinformatics-native AI agent skill library with local-first pharmacogenomics, ancestry PCA, semantic similarity, nutrigenomics, and metagenomics skills.

name: Concerto
description: Contrastive self-supervised learning framework for single-cell multimodal data integration, batch correction, and reference-query mapping.

name: CONCH
description: Vision-language foundation model for computational pathology trained with contrastive captioning on pathology image–text pairs.

name: cycleCDR
description: Interpretable cycle-consistency framework for modeling cellular responses to drug perturbations.

name: DeepAEG
description: GNN embedding + attention mechanism.

name: DeepDSC
description: Autoencoder + fully connected NN.

name: DeepDTA
description: Deep learning model using CNNs on protein sequences and drug SMILES.

name: DeepPurpose
description: Deep learning library for drug repurposing.

name: DeepSEA
description: Deep learning framework for predicting chromatin effects of sequence alterations with single-nucleotide sensitivity across thousands of chromatin features.

name: DGDRP
description: Multi-view embedding neural network.

name: DiffDock
description: Diffusion generative model for molecular docking, predicting the binding pose of small molecules to protein targets.

name: DiffSBDD
description: Equivariant diffusion model for structure-based drug design that generates molecules and binding conformations for protein targets.

name: DNABERT
description: Pre-trained bidirectional encoder for DNA sequence analysis.

name: DNABERT-2
description: Improved genome foundation model with efficient tokenization.

name: drGAT
description: Attention-based model for drug response prediction with gene explainability.

name: DrugBAN
description: Bilinear attention network for interpretable DTI prediction.

name: DRUML
description: Ensemble machine learning framework combining standard ML with deep learning to systematically rank anti-cancer drugs from proteomics and RNA-seq data.

name: DTINet
description: Network-based framework integrating heterogeneous biological data for DTI prediction.

name: Enformer
description: Transformer model predicting gene expression from DNA sequence.

name: ESM3
description: Multimodal protein language model that jointly reasons over sequence, structure, and function for generative protein design and engineering.

name: ESMFold
description: Fast protein structure prediction using language model embeddings.

name: Evo
description: Long-context genomic foundation model (up to 1M tokens).

name: EvoDiff
description: Discrete diffusion framework for protein sequence generation trained on evolutionary-scale data, supporting unconditional generation, disordered region design, and functional motif scaffolding. [ [paper-2023](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2023.09.11.556673v1) ]

name: Evolutionary Scale Modeling (ESM)
description: Protein embeddings.

name: GEARS
description: Graph-based model for predicting transcriptional responses to single and combinatorial genetic perturbations using biological priors.

name: GeneCompass
description: Large-scale foundation model integrating DNA regulatory sequences and single-cell transcriptomics from 120M+ cells across multiple species for gene regulation prediction.

name: Geneformer
description: Context-aware, attention-based deep learning model pretrained on a large corpus of single-cell transcriptomes.

name: GeneGPT
description: LLM for biomedical information, integrated with various APIs.

name: GenePT
description: Foundation LLM for single-cell data.

name: GigaPath
description: Slide-level digital pathology foundation model pretrained on 1.3 billion pathology image tokens from whole-slide images.

name: GLUE
description: Graph-Linked Unified Embedding framework for unpaired single-cell multi-omics data integration across RNA, ATAC, methylation, and protein modalities.

name: GPN (Genomic Pre-trained Network)
description: Masked language model for DNA sequences enabling zero-shot variant effect prediction without requiring functional annotations.

name: GraphDTA
description: Graph neural network–based DTI prediction using molecular graphs.

name: HiDRA
description: Hierarchical network model incorporating gene and pathway-level information for cancer drug response prediction.

name: HyenaDNA
description: Long-range genomic foundation model handling sequences up to 1M tokens with sub-quadratic attention.

name: JAMIE
description: Joint variational autoencoder for multimodal single-cell data imputation and embedding.

name: JTVAE
description: Junction tree variational autoencoder for molecular graph generation that guarantees chemical validity via a hierarchical tree decomposition.

name: MCPINN
description: Drug discovery via compound-protein interaction and machine learning.

name: MIDAS
description: Mosaic integration and differential accessibility model for single-cell multi-omics data that handles arbitrary missing-modality combinations across transcriptomics, chromatin accessibility, and proteomics.

name: MIRA
description: Probabilistic multimodal topic model jointly modeling single-cell transcriptomics and chromatin accessibility for regulatory network inference.

name: MOFA+
description: Multi-Omics Factor Analysis framework identifying shared axes of variation across bulk and single-cell datasets including RNA, ATAC, proteomics, methylation, and copy number.

name: MOFGCN
description: GCN + heterogeneous network.

name: Molecular Transformer
description: Sequence-to-sequence model for retrosynthesis prediction.

name: MolGPT
description: Transformer-based model for molecular generation.

name: MolT5
description: Language model for molecular tasks bridging text and SMILES, enabling molecule captioning and text-driven molecule generation.

name: MolTrans
description: Transformer-based DTI model leveraging molecular substructures.

name: Multigrate
description: Asymmetric multi-omics variational autoencoder for integrating single-cell data across RNA, ATAC, and protein modalities with missing-modality support.

name: MultiVI
description: Multi-modal variational autoencoder for integrating paired and unpaired single-cell RNA-seq and ATAC-seq measurements into a unified latent space.

name: MUSK
description: Vision-language foundation model for precision oncology analyzing multimodal paired text and pathology image data for biomarker prediction and retrieval.

name: NeoDTI
description: Library for drug-target interaction prediction.

name: Nicheformer
description: Foundation model for single-cell and spatial omics using a transformer architecture with positional embeddings to encode spatial cell information.

name: Nucleotide Transformer
description: Foundation model for genomic sequences across multiple species.

name: OmegaFold
description: High-resolution de novo protein structure prediction from sequence.

name: OpenFold
description: Trainable, memory-efficient open-source reproduction of AlphaFold2 enabling custom protein structure prediction workflows.

name: PaccMannRL
description: Reinforcement learning-based generative model for de novo hit-like anticancer molecule design from transcriptomic data.

name: PathomicFusion
description: Integrated framework fusing histopathology and genomic features via CNN, GNN, and attention gating for cancer diagnosis and prognosis.

name: Phikon
description: ViT-based pathology foundation model pretrained with iBOT self-supervision on TCGA whole-slide images.

name: PLIP
description: Vision-language foundation model for pathology trained with contrastive learning on pathology image–text pairs for image classification and text-to-image retrieval.

name: PORPOISE
description: Pan-cancer integrative histology-genomic analysis framework using multimodal deep learning for patient stratification.

name: PRNet
description: Deep generative model for predicting transcriptional responses to novel chemical perturbations for drug discovery.

name: ProGen2
description: Protein language model trained on diverse protein families for sequence generation and fitness prediction.

name: ProteinMPNN
description: Deep learning model for protein sequence design given backbone structure.

name: ProtTrans
description: Suite of protein language models (ProtBERT, ProtT5, ProtXLNet) trained on billions of protein sequences from UniRef and BFD.

name: RECOVER
description: Machine learning framework for predicting synergistic drug combination responses across cell lines.

name: REINVENT
description: Reinforcement learning for de novo drug design.

name: ReLeaSE
description: Deep reinforcement learning framework for de novo drug design combining a generative and predictive model.

name: RFdiffusion
description: Generative model for protein backbone design using diffusion.

name: RoseTTAFold
description: Three-track neural network for protein structure prediction.

name: SaProt
description: Structure-aware protein language model using structure-aware tokens that encode both sequence and backbone geometry for improved function prediction.

name: SATURN
description: Transformer-based model integrating gene expression and protein sequences via a protein language model to learn unified multi-species cell embeddings.

name: scArches
description: Transfer learning framework for mapping new single-cell datasets onto pre-trained reference atlases across batches, conditions, and modalities.

name: scBERT
description: BERT-based foundation model pretrained on large-scale scRNA-seq data for cell type annotation.

name: scButterfly
description: Dual-aligned variational autoencoder for single-cell cross-modality translation between paired and unpaired multiomics data.

name: scFoundation
description: Large-scale foundation model for single-cell gene expression, enabling multiple downstream tasks.

name: scGPT
description: Transformer-based foundation model pretrained on millions of single-cell profiles.

name: scGPT-spatial
description: Extension of scGPT for spatial transcriptomics with continual pretraining and a mixture-of-experts decoder for spatial gene expression analysis.

name: scMulan
description: Single-cell multi-omic language model pretrained on ~10M cells spanning transcriptomics, epigenomics, and proteomics for cross-omics transfer tasks.

name: scPair
description: Bidirectional feedforward network for single-cell multimodal analysis with cross-modality prediction leveraging single-cell atlases.

name: scPRINT
description: Pretrained on 50M cells for scRNA-seq denoising & zero imputation.

name: Sei
description: Sequence-to-function framework learning a genome-wide regulatory activity code from DNA sequences for variant effect prediction.

name: SpatialGlue
description: Graph attention network for spatial multi-omics integration jointly embedding spatial transcriptomics with chromatin accessibility or proteomics.

name: TargetDiff
description: 3D equivariant diffusion model for structure-based drug design.

name: TGSA
description: Tumor gene set and attention-based model leveraging biological pathway knowledge for drug response prediction.

name: TOAD
description: Tumor Origin Assessment via Deep-learning; weakly-supervised multi-task model predicting cancer primary origin from H&E whole-slide images.

name: TOSICA
description: Transformer-based framework for one-stop interpretable cell-type annotation supporting cross-dataset and cross-species transfer.

name: totalVI
description: Probabilistic framework for joint analysis of paired scRNA-seq and protein (CITE-seq) data enabling multi-modal cell state representation across single-cell datasets.

name: TransformerCPI
description: CPI prediction using Transformer.

name: TranSiGen
description: Dual-VAE architecture for ligand-based virtual screening, drug response prediction, and drug repurposing using chemical-induced transcriptional profiles.

name: UCE
description: Universal Cell Embeddings: zero-shot single-cell embedding model trained on 36M cells across species, tissues, and assays without fine-tuning.

name: UNI
description: General-purpose self-supervised pathology foundation model trained on 100K+ whole-slide images for diverse computational pathology tasks.

name: UnitedNet
description: Interpretable multi-task deep neural network for single-cell multi-omics integration spanning transcriptomics, chromatin accessibility, and proteomics.

name: Virchow
description: Million-slide digital pathology foundation model using a vision transformer and self-supervised distillation for tile-level pathology image representation.

name: Biopython
description: Collection of Python tools for biological computation including sequence analysis, structure parsing, and database access.

name: CaSpER
description: CNV identification and visualization by integrative analysis of single-cell or bulk RNA-seq data.

name: CellCharter
description: Identification and characterization of spatial cell niches from spatial transcriptomics using VAEs and Gaussian mixture models.

name: CellChat
description: Inference and analysis of cell-cell communication ligand-receptor networks from single-cell transcriptomics data.

name: CellTypist
description: Automated cell type annotation for scRNA-seq.

name: ChatSpatial
description: MCP server for spatial transcriptomics analysis via natural language.

name: Chemistry Development Kit
description: Cheminformatics software & machine learning tools.

name: COMMOT
description: Optimal transport-based framework for screening cell-cell communication in spatial transcriptomics.

name: DeepChem
description: Deep learning library for drug discovery, quantum chemistry, and materials science.

name: DeepTalk
description: Graph attention network for deciphering cell-cell communication from spatial transcriptomics data.

name: DoubletFinder
description: Machine learning approach for detecting multiplet (doublet) artifacts in single-cell RNA-seq data.

name: FlashDeconv
description: High-performance spatial transcriptomics deconvolution (~1M spots in ~3 min).

name: GROMACS
description: Molecular dynamics simulation package for biochemical molecules.

name: Harmony
description: Fast and scalable integration of single-cell data across datasets, conditions, technologies, and species.

name: kallisto
description: Near-optimal RNA-seq quantification using pseudoalignment for fast transcript abundance estimation.

name: LINGER
description: Neural network for gene regulatory network inference from single-cell multiome (RNA+ATAC-seq) data with bulk data pretraining.

name: MDAnalysis
description: Python library for analyzing and altering molecular dynamics simulation trajectories.

name: MOGONET
description: Multi-omics graph convolutional network framework for patient classification and biomarker identification.

name: Monocle3
description: Single-cell trajectory analysis tool for learning developmental trajectories and ordering cells in pseudotime.

name: NCEM
description: GNN-based model for learning intercellular communication from spatial graphs of cells.

name: Numbat
description: Haplotype-aware copy number variation inference from single-cell RNA-seq using hidden Markov models.

name: OpenMM
description: High-performance toolkit for molecular simulation and GPU-accelerated MD.

name: RDKit
description: Cheminformatics software & machine learning toolkit.

name: Scanpy
description: Python library for scRNA-seq analysis.

name: SCENIC
description: Single-cell regulatory network inference and clustering linking transcription factors to co-expressed gene modules.

name: sciPENN
description: RNN-based method for simultaneous protein expression prediction, uncertainty estimation, and cell-type label transfer from CITE-seq and scRNA-seq data.

name: scVelo
description: RNA velocity estimation for single-cell transcriptomics, inferring the direction and speed of cell differentiation.

name: scvi-tools
description: Probabilistic models for single-cell omics data analysis.

name: Seurat
description: R library for scRNA-seq analysis.

name: Squidpy
description: Python library for spatial single-cell analysis.

name: STAGATE
description: Adaptive graph attention auto-encoder for spatial domain identification in spatial transcriptomics.

name: STAR
description: Ultrafast universal RNA-seq aligner with support for spliced alignment and single-cell quantification via STARsolo.

name: TIGON
description: Neural optimal transport method for reconstructing growth and dynamic trajectories from single-cell transcriptomics.
